name: English Crumpets- :)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour and salt into a mixing bowl.
2. Gently warm the milk to just hand hot and sprinkle on the dried yeast. Leave to stand for 10 or 15 minutes until frothy.
3. Add the yeast mixture to the flour and beat to a smooth batter. Cover with a damp cloth and leave to rise in a warm place for 45 minutes, or until the batter has doubled in size.
4. Dissolve the bicarb in 15 milliliters of warm water and beat it into the batter. Cover again and leave to stand for a further 20 minutes.
5. Place a 3-inch metal pastry cutter into a hot greased fry pan. Pour in about one tablespoon of the batter to cover the base thinly.
6. Cook until the top is set and the bubbles have burst. Remove it from the ring, turn the crumpet over, and cook the other side for 2 or 3 minutes only. It should just color slightly.
7. Cool on a wire rack. Eat them now, or leave to go cold and toast them until brown on both sides. Spread with lots of good butter and enjoy.
